macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Decline Sharply on Tariff and AI Fears
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.04% to 6,837, Dow Jones dropped 1.66% to 48,804, and Nasdaq shed 1.13% to 22,627 amid concerns over new Trump tariffs and AI disruption risks to software stocks like IBM, Salesforce, and Intuit.[1][2]

items.title: S&P 500 Forms Bearish Rising Wedge Pattern
items.summary: The S&P 500 retreated over 1% to 6,837, forming a rising wedge and bearish divergence, with potential downside to 6,530 support; outlook bearish unless it breaks all-time high.[1][5]

items.title: TSX Composite Slips Amid Trade Uncertainty
items.summary: Canada's S&P/TSX Composite fell 41 points to 33,777 after intraday highs, dragged by tech, industrials, and financials; gold miners like Fortuna Mining and Lundin Gold rose over 6% on strengthening metals.[3]

items.title: Gold Surges 2.5% as Safe Haven on Tariff and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Gold broke above $5,100 resistance to close near $2,230, up 2.5%, driven by US tariff news, reactions from trading partners, and potential escalation with Iran.[2]

items.title: US Stocks Steady Tuesday After Monday Slump
items.summary: US markets rebounded slightly on Tuesday with S&P 500 up 0.1% and Dow up 0.4% or 219 points as of mid-morning, following Monday's AI-driven decline; Nvidia earnings anticipated tomorrow.[6][4]

items.title: Software Stocks Tumble on AI Disruption Fears
items.summary: IBM had its worst day since 2000 after Anthropic's COBOL modernization hint; Salesforce, Workday, Intuit continued falling; chip stocks overextended while software hits support.[1][5]

items.title: Winpak Shares Drop on Weak Q4 Earnings
items.summary: Winpak fell 3.4% to $50.27 after softer Q4 and full-year 2025 profitability; among TSX's worst performers alongside goeasy, Shopify, and others down over 5.8%.[3]

items.title: Treasury Yields Fall as Investors Seek Safety
items.summary: US 2-year Treasury yield dropped 4 bps to 3.483%, 10-year fell 5.4 bps to 4.029% amid equity retreat and bond rotation.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Faces $465 Million Sell-Off Amid Tariff Uncertainty and Weak U.S. GDP
items.summary: Renewed tariff uncertainty and disappointing U.S. GDP data triggered a $465 million cryptocurrency sell-off. This reflects broader market vulnerability due to risk-off sentiment.

items.title: Senate Resumes Talks on Crypto Market Structure Bill
items.summary: Discussions have restarted on the Senate's proposed crypto market structure legislation amid ongoing market pressures. This regulatory development aims to provide clearer frameworks for digital assets.

items.title: February 2026 Crypto Correction Deepens with Bitcoin Dropping Below $61,000
items.summary: Digital assets extended their correction in February, with Bitcoin briefly falling below $61,000 during a sell-off on February 5-6, erasing nearly half of its peak value from October 2025. High trading volumes suggest the aggressive drawdown phase may be nearing exhaustion.

items.title: 2026 Predicted as Defining Year for Stablecoins in On-Chain Finance
items.summary: Curve Finance founder Michael Egorov forecasts 2026 as a pivotal year for stablecoins becoming core financial infrastructure, with accelerating institutional experiments and a focus on security amid rising hacker threats. Consolidation in major networks like Ethereum and Solana is also expected.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Transitions from Experimental Phase to Core Business Operations
items.summary: Enterprise AI is moving beyond pilot projects to become integrated into core business processes, requiring organizations to shift focus from innovation metrics to reliability, graceful failure handling, and risk management as AI becomes the operating model.

items.title: Federal Reserve Examines AI's Impact on Monetary Policy and Interest Rates
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa D. Cook discusses how AI-driven business investment in data centers and chips may affect the neutral rate of interest and monetary policy, noting that current neutral rates may be higher than pre-pandemic levels due to strong AI-related aggregate demand.

items.title: Teachers Report AI Fueling Crisis in Student Critical Thinking Skills
items.summary: Educators warn that student reliance on AI tools is creating a measurable decline in critical thinking, reading comprehension, and writing abilities, with students entering 'passenger mode' and experiencing 'digitally induced amnesia' as they offload cognitive tasks to AI platforms.

items.title: Agentic AI Introduces Expanded Security Threats in Mobile Networks
items.summary: Ericsson identifies ten key security considerations for telecommunications operators as Agentic AI evolves, highlighting new attack vectors including evasion, poisoning, and inference attacks that require enhanced identity and access management controls.

items.title: Only 45% of Organizations Successfully Embedding AI into Workflows
items.summary: EXL's 2026 Enterprise AI Study reveals a significant gap between AI ambition and execution, with less than half of organizations achieving desired outcomes and only 45% of workflows having AI embedded, highlighting challenges in scaling AI across enterprises.

items.title: Biomarker Discovery Advances Through AI and Personalized Medicine
items.summary: New developments in artificial intelligence are enabling breakthrough discoveries in biomarker identification, supporting the advancement of personalized medicine approaches.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Announces Surprise Treehouse Stream for Mario and Pokemon
items.summary: Nintendo revealed a Nintendo Treehouse Live Presentation on February 24 at 10PM GMT, showcasing hands-on gameplay of Super Mario Bros. Wonder Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park expansion and Pokemon Pokopia, both upcoming Switch 2 exclusives.

items.title: New Game Releases This Week Including Towerborne, City Hunter, and Fallout 4 Anniversary Edition
items.summary: Upcoming releases feature Towerborne on PC/PS5/Series X|S (Feb 26), City Hunter on PC (Feb 25), Fallout 4: Anniversary Edition on Switch 2 (Feb 24), and others like The Disney Afternoon Collection and No Sleep for Kaname Date.

items.title: Amazon Shutting Down King of Meat After Six Months
items.summary: Amazon Game Studios and developer Glowmade are closing the UGC dungeon brawler King of Meat, launched on October 2, after just six months.

items.title: Dead by Daylight Announces All-Kill Public Test Build
items.summary: Dead by Daylight has launched a Steam Public Test Build for its new All-Kill release on February 24, 2026.

items.title: Microsoft's New Gaming CEO Asha Sharma Rejects 'Bad AI' and Promises Human Focus
items.summary: New Microsoft Gaming head Asha Sharma vows to avoid 'soulless AI slop' amid concerns over Xbox's future under new leadership.

items.title: Concerns Rise Over Xbox's Future with New Leadership
items.summary: Commentary highlights troubling prospects for Xbox under new heads Asha Sharma and Matt Booty, speculating a shift away from hardware like Sega.

items.title: GDC 2026 State of the Industry Highlights Layoffs and Steam Deck Growth
items.summary: The GDC 2026 report notes widespread layoffs impacting job prospects, alongside strong momentum for Steam Deck and other developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: AMD and Meta Announce Multi-Year Partnership for 6 Gigawatts of GPU Infrastructure
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices and Meta Platforms revealed a partnership to deploy up to 6 gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs, with the first gigawatt using custom MI450-based GPUs and 6th Gen EPYC CPUs starting in late 2026. The deal includes performance-based warrants for up to 160 million AMD shares.

items.title: Accenture Acquires Avanseus' AI Solution for Telecom Network Optimization
items.summary: Accenture acquired an advanced AI solution from Avanseus to enhance its platform for network planning, engineering, and optimization, aiding communications companies in achieving autonomous networks and improving operational efficiency.
